questions.prompt: A team stored user session data in KV. Sessions updated in one region weren't visible in another for ~60s, breaking user experience. Right fix?

choices.text: Move sessions to D1 (strong consistency on writes). Keep KV for read-only config (where ~60s eventual consistency is fine). KV is great for global eventually-consistent data; for anything where stale reads cause bugs, pick a strongly-consistent primitive.

questions.explanation: KV's eventual consistency (~60s) is a feature for some workloads (cheap global reads) and a bug for others (sessions, anything where stale reads break UX). Match the consistency model to the workload. D1 for strong consistency; KV for tolerable lag; external Postgres for high-write transactional.

choices.text: Single-threaded, addressable-by-ID stateful actors. Each instance handles requests sequentially with no race conditions, holds persistent state (`state.storage`), supports WebSockets. Designed for many small instances (one per room/document/user). Perfect for real-time coordination of per-entity state.

questions.explanation: DOs are stateful, single-threaded, addressable. The right fit is many small instances each coordinating real-time state (collaboration room, game match, per-user counter). Not a general DB replacement; a specialized primitive for coordination.
